title: Mermeia

{{Quote|I know you're searching for me. Searching, searching. I am here. My voice is for you alone. I am found in your eyes only. I exist for you. I am in your mind as you create me. Oh, yes… I can feel my creation.''" [giggles] "''I'm getting your message. Are you getting mine?|Mermeia|audio=MermeiaCreation-SWHS.flac|<ref name="Holiday Special" />}}
'''Mermeia''' was a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] fantasy [[Gender/Legends|woman]] who existed within [[virtual reality]] as an erotic [[entertainer]] for users of [[Mind evaporator/Legends|mind evaporators]] and [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojectors]]. When accessed via the former device, she was a product of the user's deepest desires, and she appeared to them as created by their [[Brain/Legends|mind]]. On at least two occasions, she appeared as a slim woman with the features of a [[Human/Legends|Human]]. Mermeia [[Music/Legends|sang]], danced, and flirted with those who accessed her, and the experience resulted in considerable pleasure for user and entertainer alike. Shortly after [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Mermeia danced for an enthralled, holoprojector-using [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]], and in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], she sang the song "[[This Minute Now]]" to [[Attichitcuk/Legends|Attichitcuk]], an elderly and excitable [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] who viewed her performance through a mind evaporator.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I don't need to ask how you find me. You see, I am your fantasy. I am your experience. So experience me. I am your pleasure. So enjoy me. This is our moment together in time. And we might turn this moment into an eternity.|Mermeia, to Attichitcuk|audio=MermeiaExperience-SWHS.flac|<ref name="Holiday Special" />}}
[[File:Mermeia-SWGTCG.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Mermeia dances for an entranced Twi'lek.]]
Mermeia was a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] [[Gender/Legends|woman]]<ref name="Bad Feeling" /> and a private [[entertainer]]<ref name="Holiday Special" /> who existed within [[virtual reality]].<ref name="Insider 23">{{InsiderCite|23|The Star Wars Holiday Special (article)|''The Star Wars Holiday Special''}}</ref> Interactions with and performances by her were accessible to users of both [[Mind evaporator/Legends|mind evaporators]]<ref name="Holiday Special" /> and [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojectors]].<ref name="Thought as Action">{{SWGTCG|set=Threat of the Conqueror|cardname=Thought as Action}}</ref> When a user inserted a certain [[cassette]] into a [[Brain/Legends|mind evaporator]] and interfaced with the machine<ref name="Holiday Special" /> for erotic purposes,<ref name="Insider 23" /> a fantasy of theirs would be conjured from their deepest desires and appear before them. Mermeia was one such fantasy.<ref name="Holiday Special" /> Also accessible via holoprojectors, Mermeia appeared as a hologram to a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]<ref name="Thought as Action" /> shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> an engagement fought in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The Twi'lek accessed a portable holoprojector in a public [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] and watched intently as Mermeia danced.<ref name="Thought as Action" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
Around the same time, a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] bought a mind evaporator on the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Holiday (disambiguation)|Holiday]] [[Life Day/Legends|Life Day]], and interacted with Mermeia after bringing the device home.<ref name="SWG:PC">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=item|Proton Chair}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="Leland Holiday">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=870#15778940|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-10-12|time=6:13 PM}}</ref> Mermeia appeared before a [[Color/Legends|colorful]] kaleidoscopic background to the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Attichitcuk/Legends|Attichitcuk]], who inserted her cassette into a mind evaporator while in the living room of [[Chewbacca's home|his home]], a [[Tree/Legends|treetop]] dwelling<ref name="Holiday Special" /> located in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Rwookrrorro/Legends|Rwookrrorro]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].<ref name="Tyrant's Test">''[[Tyrant's Test]]''</ref> Mermeia flirted with Attichitcuk, who viewed her through his mind evaporator's helmet, and she told him that she was a creation of his desires, which heavily excited the Wookiee. She then sang the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[This Minute Now]]," which spoke of a longing for her [[Time/Legends|time]] with Attichitcuk to last forever. After the song concluded, the satisfied Wookiee removed the helmet and returned to his [[Chewbacca's family/Legends|family's]] preparations for a celebration of Life Day.<ref name="Holiday Special" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Oh, oh! We are excited, aren't we? Just relax. Just relax. Yes! Now… we can have a good time, can't we?|Mermeia becomes giddy with excitement|audio=MermeiaGoodTime-SWHS.flac|<ref name="Holiday Special" />}}
[[File:MermeiaPerforming.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Mermeia existed within virtual reality.]]
Mermeia was the representation of mind evaporator users' desires, and she existed<ref name="Holiday Special" /> within the realm of virtual reality<ref name="Insider 23" /> as a fantasy for their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] only. Mermeia appeared to them as their mind created her; to Attichitcuk, she had purplish-white [[Hair/Legends|hair]], gray eyes, and brown skin.<ref name="Holiday Special" /> Both Attichitcuk and the holoprojector-using Twi'lek saw her as a slim [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] figure with [[Human/Legends|Human]]-like features and long hair.<ref name="Holiday Special" /><ref name="Thought as Action" /> To Attichitcuk, she wore a long gown,<ref name="Holiday Special" /> but to the Twi'lek, she sported an outfit that revealed her [[Limb/Legends|legs]].<ref name="Thought as Action" /> In the Wookiee's fantasy, multiple incarnations of Mermeia appeared simultaneously. He was also able to rewind her cassette and experience a single moment with her multiple times.<ref name="Holiday Special" />

During Attichitcuk's experience, Mermeia stood before a kaleidoscopic background of shifting colors. Her appearance was heralded first by dancing [[Unidentified swimming species|water creatures]], and subsequently by her voice, which teased the eager Wookiee. She became giddy when Attichitcuk's desires gave her holographic form. Mermeia could feel the Wookiee's emotions, and his excitement at seeing her gave her considerable pleasure. She flirted with Attichitcuk by calling him adorable, telling him to enjoy her, and promising that they would have a good time together. A song that she sang told of her desire for that time to be eternal. Attichitcuk thoroughly enjoyed the<ref name="Holiday Special" /> erotic experience.<ref name="Insider 23" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The show has many musical numbers, one of the most bizarre of which is when Itchy throws on the helmet of his 'mind evaporator' and has an erotic virtual reality experience (this thing was years ahead of its time) with Diahann Carroll, who tells him 'You're adorable. I am your fantasy, I am your pleasure. This is our moment in time.' Woah ''[sic]''! Kinky space love!|Jon Bradley Snyder|<ref name="Insider 23" />}}
The character of Mermeia was created for ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'',<ref name="Holiday Special" /> a [[1978]] television [[Wikipedia:Variety show|variety show]] [[Wikipedia:Spin-off (media)|spin-off]] of the [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|original ''Star Wars'' film]], released [[1977|one year prior]].<ref name="Comedy Hour">{{WebCite|url=http://www.vanityfair.com/magazine/2008/12/star_wars_special200812|text=The Han Solo Comedy Hour! (page 1 of 3)|work=Vanity Fair|archivedate=20081223164238}}</ref> Mermeia was portrayed by actress and singer [[Diahann Carroll]], who was cast in the program by producers [[Ken Welch|Ken]] and [[Mitzie Welch]]. The Welches were seeking high-profile celebrities to perform the ''Holiday Special''{{'s}} various song-and-dance numbers;<ref name="script and directors">{{SWArchive|url=movies/saga/20081117.html|text=Holiday Special: Script and Directors}}</ref> [[Wikipedia:Cher|Cher]] was originally cast as the character, though she had to decline due to a surgery.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.earwolf.com/episode/bruce-vilanch|text=Gilbert Gottfried's Amazing Colossal Podcast #220|date=2018-08-12|work=[https://www.earwolf.com/ Earwold Podcast]|archivedate=20190528024109}}</ref> Carroll was ultimately cast in the role, partially because the program's parent [[CBS]] network wanted [[Wikipedia:Black people|black people]] in the ''Holiday Special'', as some controversy surrounded the original film's lack of non-white characters at that time. After Carroll was brought aboard the project, director [[David Acomba]] puzzled over what kind of role she should play,<ref name="script and directors" /> but the Welches eventually wrote her the song "This Minute Now."<ref name="Comedy Hour 3">{{WebCite|url=http://www.vanityfair.com/magazine/2008/12/star_wars_special200812?currentPage=3|text=The Han Solo Comedy Hour! (page 3 of 3)|work=Vanity Fair|archivedate=20081225130516}}</ref>

[[File:Softcoreporn.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Mermeia's scene in ''The Star Wars Holiday Special'' was intended to be softcore pornography.]]
Mermeia's skit was intended by its writers to be [[Wikipedia:Softcore pornography|softcore pornography]] that would pass CBS's censors,<ref name="Comedy Hour 3" /> despite the creative team's general aim to appeal to family audiences.<ref name="Starlog">([[February]] [[1979]]). "Star Wars" Invades TV. [[Wikipedia:Starlog|Starlog]] magazine number 19.</ref> In the ''Holiday Special''{{'s}} script, when Attichitcuk is given the cassette by the [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Saun Dann]], Dann is written "as if he is giving (Attichitcuk) the most X-rated piece of literature in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]." The script further compares Dann's actions to those of a dirty old man, and later states that Attichitcuk is "groggy with satisfaction" following his mind evaporator experience.<ref name="script">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarsholidayspecial.com/text/script.htm|text=The Fourth Draft Script - 09/13/78|work=The Star Wars Holiday Special|archivedate=20040130084012}}</ref> The perceived bizarre sexual nature of Mermeia's skit has been noted by critics of the ''Holiday Special''—Scott Stinson of the [[Wikipedia:National Post|''National Post'']] has poked fun at Attichitcuk's not-at-all-appropriate-for-the-Holidays "eroticized dream;"<ref name="National Post">{{WebCite|url=http://www.nationalpost.com/opinion/columnists/force+with+this+season/5787160/story.html|text=May the force be with you this season|author=Scott Stinson|date=2011-11-30|work=[[Wikipedia:National Post|National Post]]|archiveurl=https://archive.is/oZiUQ}}</ref> Adam Feldman of the [[Wikipedia:Time Out (company)|''Time Out'']] publishing company has commented on the outlandishness of Attichitcuk's interaction with a "virtual reality sex tape;"<ref name="ABC">{{WebCite|url=http://abcnews.go.com/Nightline/story?id=4034365&page=2|text=Holiday Specials Gone Bad|author=John Berman and Ted Gernstein|work=abcnews.go.com|date=2007-12-20|archivedate=20180802193011}}</ref> and Frank DiGiacomo of [[Wikipedia:Vanity Fair (magazine)|''Vanity Fair'' magazine]] has described one of Mermeia's moans of excitement as an "orgasmic shudder."<ref name="Comedy Hour 3" /> ''[[Star Wars]]'' author [[Jon Bradley Snyder]] has additionally referred to Mermeia's relationship with Attichitcuk as "kinky [[Realspace/Legends|space]] love."<ref name="Insider 23" /> Despite the suggestive nature of the scene, ''Star Wars'' author [[Alex Newborn]] has opined that Carroll is the most talented soloist of the program,<ref name="Bad Feeling" /> and ''Holiday Special'' writer [[Bruce Vilanch]] has referred to her on-set presence as "sexy and gorgeous."<ref name="farce2">{{WebCite|url=http://articles.latimes.com/2008/nov/16/entertainment/ca-starwars16/2|text=May the farce be with you (page 2 of 3)|author=Donald Liebenson|date=2008-11-16|work=Los Angeles Times|archivedate=20160911152459}}</ref>

Although Mermeia is not named in the ''Holiday Special'',<ref name="Holiday Special" /> she is identified in its script<ref name="script" /> and in ''[[I Have a Bad Feeling About This!]]'', a [[2008]] article published in the [[Star Wars Insider 106|106th issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine.<ref name="Bad Feeling" /> Mermeia is also featured on "Thought as Action," a trading card released with the [[2010]] ''[[Threat of the Conqueror]]'' set of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]''. The card misspells her name as "Mermia,"<ref name="Thought as Action" /> an error that also appears once in the ''Holiday Special''{{'s}} script.<ref name="script" /> Author [[Kevin J. Anderson]] used Mermeia as visual inspiration when designing [[Qwi Xux]],<ref name="Diana Ross">{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucasfan.com/swtv/index3.html|text=Star Wars Holiday Special Opinions|work=[https://web.archive.org/web/19981212015351/http://lucasfan.com LucasFan.com]|archivedate=20200120144211|nolive=1}}</ref> a [[Bird/Legends|birdlike]] [[Omwati/Legends|Omwati]] character who first appeared in his [[1994]] novel ''[[Jedi Search]]''.<ref name="Jedi Search">''[[Jedi Search]]''</ref> When discussing Xux's creation in an interview, Anderson referred to Mermeia as a "[[Wikipedia:Diana Ross|Diana Ross]] glittery-feathery [[Alien/Legends|alien]]."<ref name="Diana Ross" />
Few details about the character have been given in ''Star Wars'' [[canon]], but the ''Holiday Special''{{'s}} script suggests that her scene could perhaps take place beneath the surface of a large lake.<ref name="script" /> In the ''Holiday Special'' itself, when presenting the cassette tape to Attichitcuk, Saun Dann tells the Wookiee "It's hard to describe. It's… wow!"<ref name="Holiday Special" /> Although the name "wow" does not actually appear in ''Star Wars'' canon, Mermeia is called a "Holographic Wow" on Diahann Carroll's [http://www.diahanncarroll.net/film_credits.htm website]<ref name="Carroll's website">{{WebCite|url=http://www.diahanncarroll.net/film_credits.htm|text=Film Credits|work=[http://www.diahanncarroll.net Diahann Carroll Official Website]|archivedate=20170223081152}}</ref> and the fan site [[Starwarsholidayspecial.com]].<ref name="SKot">{{WebCite|url=http://www.starwarsholidayspecial.com/characters/mermeia.htm|text=Mermeia|work=[[Starwarsholidayspecial.com|The Star Wars Holiday Special]]|archivedate=20170709193528}}</ref>

Proton chairs appeared as decorations for player's house in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:PC" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Proton chairs were added to the game with the release of the revamped [[W:c:swg:Life Day|Life Day]] event in [[December]] [[2008]].<ref name="SonyCite-Friday Feature – ''Corellia Times'' Exclusive: Life Day Revealed">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=68147&month=122008|text=Friday Feature – ''Corellia Times'' Exclusive: Life Day Revealed}}</ref> Mermeia's voice could be heard when the player character sat on a proton chair.<ref name="SWG:PC" />
